pattern: Since a qualifying substring needs equal vowels and consonants, only even-length windows can ever count, so running vowel totals turn every start/end pair into an O(1) check instead of rescanning characters.
hints: A substring can only possibly qualify if its length is even, because equal vowel and consonant counts must add up to the whole length.
Keep a running total of vowels seen so far so the vowel count of any substring can be found by subtracting two running totals instead of rescanning its characters.

Walk every pair of start and end points whose gap is even, derive vowels and consonants for that window from the running totals, and count it whenever they are equal and their product divides evenly by k.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two overlapping beautiful substrings
statement example, three beautiful substrings with k=1
statement example, no vowels at all
single character can never be beautiful, length is odd
all vowels, only even-length windows are even candidates
a large k that rarely divides the vowel-consonant product
alternating vowel and consonant letters produce many overlapping matches
maximum k with a string that has no vowels, always zero

stress.description: random lowercase string at the maximum length with a random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    return [s, k]
